data.title: Hillman Solutions (HLMN) EPS Surge Of 134.9% Tests Bullish Margin Narratives
data.description:
Hillman Solutions (HLMN) has wrapped up FY 2025 with fourth quarter revenue of US$365.1 million and basic EPS of roughly US$0.01, rounding out a trailing twelve month picture that shows US$1.6 billion of revenue and EPS of about US$0.20. Over the past six reported quarters, revenue has moved from US$349.6 million in Q4 2024 to US$365.1 million in Q4 2025. Quarterly EPS has ranged from a small loss of about US$0.00 in early 2025 to US$0.12 in Q3 before easing back to roughly US$0.01 in the latest quarter. This sets up a story where improving margins and earnings quality are front and center for investors.

See our full analysis for Hillman Solutions.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to line these results up against the key stories around Hillman, highlighting where the data backs the prevailing narratives and where it pushes investors to rethink them.

See what the community is saying about Hillman Solutions

Advertisement

TTM net margin at 2.6% puts profit quality in focus

  • Over the last twelve months Hillman generated US$40.3 million of net income on US$1.6b of revenue, which works out to a 2.6% net margin compared with 1.2% in the prior year period cited in the analysis.
  • Consensus narrative sees efficiency investments and acquisitions as key margin drivers. The current 2.6% margin sits between that story and the risk list:
    • On the supportive side, analysts point to omni channel investments, automation and the dual faucet sourcing plan as tools that can help margins, and the move from 1.2% to 2.6% in the margin figure is consistent with that direction of travel.
    • On the other hand, the same consensus view flags tariffs, integration of lower margin deals and high debt as margin headwinds, and a 2.6% margin leaves less room to absorb cost or volume pressure compared with companies that run at higher profitability.

EPS up 134.9% year over year tests the bullish case

  • Trailing twelve month basic EPS is US$0.20, and the analysis data says that is 134.9% higher than a year earlier, while the quarterly pattern in 2025 ranged from about US$0.00 in Q1 to US$0.12 in Q3 before easing to roughly US$0.01 in Q4.
  • Bulls argue that supply chain shifts and pricing power can keep EPS growing, and the current figures both support and challenge that view:
    • As a supporting point, five year earnings growth is described at an average of 63.3% per year and trailing EPS growth is very strong at 134.9%, which lines up with the bullish idea that margin structure can improve as sourcing moves to countries like Vietnam, Thailand and India and as pricing on repair focused products holds.
    • As a question mark for that bullish view, quarterly EPS for FY 2025 still swung from a small loss in Q1 to US$0.12 in Q3 and then back to roughly US$0.01 in Q4, so the path to the bullish earnings targets discussed in the narrative would need more consistent delivery than this recent pattern alone shows.
If you want to see how bullish analysts connect this EPS track record to their longer term story for the business, check out the latest market case for Hillman in 🐂 Hillman Solutions Bull Case.

P/E of 43.2x and weak interest cover echo bearish worries

  • At a share price of US$8.86 the trailing P/E is 43.2x, above the US Machinery industry average of 30.1x and below the peer group average of 50.7x, while the same dataset flags that interest payments are not well covered by earnings.
  • Bears focus on balance sheet strain and retailer dependence, and the current valuation and risk flags speak directly to that:
    • On the risk side, critics highlight that high debt and ongoing capital needs could pressure earnings if volumes soften, and the comment that interest expense is not well covered lines up with concerns about financial flexibility alongside relatively thin 2.6% margins.
    • At the same time, the stock is described as trading about 56.3% below a DCF fair value of US$20.24 and the only allowed analyst target of US$12.09 sits above the current US$8.86 price, so the bearish worries about leverage are being weighed by a market that is also assigning a higher multiple than the broader industry.
Skeptics point to that mix of a higher P/E and weaker interest cover as a key reason for caution, so if you want to see the full cautious take that builds on these numbers, have a look at 🐻 Hillman Solutions Bear Case.

data.title: HF Sinclair (DINO) Q3 EPS Rebound Tests Bearish Volatility Narrative
data.description:
HF Sinclair (DINO) has just posted its FY 2025 third quarter numbers, with revenue of US$7.3b and basic EPS of US$2.16, set against trailing 12 month earnings growth of 18.7%. The company has seen revenue move from US$7.2b in Q3 2024 to US$7.3b in Q3 2025, while quarterly EPS shifted from a loss of US$0.40 to EPS of US$2.16. The trailing net profit margin sits at 1.5% versus 1.1% last year, giving investors a clearer view of how the earnings profile is shaping up. Taken together, these results point to margins that are still relatively thin but moving in a direction that keeps profitability firmly in focus for shareholders.

See our full analysis for HF Sinclair.

With the headline figures on the table, the next step is to see how these results line up with the widely held narratives around HF Sinclair, highlighting where the numbers support the story and where they start to push back against it.

See what the community is saying about HF Sinclair

Advertisement

Profit swings show how cyclical refining can be

  • HF Sinclair moved from a net loss of US$213.3 million in Q4 2024 and US$3 million in Q1 2025 to net income of US$207.0 million in Q2 2025 and US$403.0 million in Q3 2025, highlighting how quickly profitability can shift within a few quarters.
  • Bears focus on that earlier loss making stretch and argue that refining earnings can remain volatile, yet the recent US$403.0 million profit tests the bearish view that pressure on margins will necessarily translate into persistently weak results.
    • The bearish narrative points to a first quarter net loss and pressure on refining EBITDA, which is consistent with the US$3 million loss in Q1 2025, but the swing to over US$200 million and then over US$400 million of profit suggests that segment performance can rebound when conditions are more favorable.
    • For a cautious investor, the pattern here is not one way traffic; it is a reminder that the same business model that produced losses in late 2024 and early 2025 is also capable of delivering solid profits when margins and volumes support it.

When you see profit move from losses to over US$400 million in a few quarters, it underlines how quickly the story can change in this kind of business, which is exactly what critics of the sector worry about.

🐻 HF Sinclair Bear Case

TTM earnings growth and 1.5% margin back the bullish case

  • Over the last twelve months HF Sinclair earned US$391.0 million of net income on US$26.9b of revenue, giving a 1.5% net margin and 18.7% earnings growth compared with the prior year, which is well above the roughly 2% per year growth rate over the past five years.
  • Bulls argue that tighter supply and resilient fuel demand support stronger and more durable profitability, and the combination of a 1.5% trailing margin and 18.7% earnings growth gives some numerical backing to that optimism, even though margins are still thin.
    • Supporters highlight that earnings are flagged as high quality, so the US$391.0 million of trailing profit is not just an accounting quirk but reflects underlying operations, which fits the bullish story about improved utilization, efficiency projects, and renewables starting to contribute.
    • At the same time, the margin is still only 1.5% on US$26.9b of sales, so while that aligns with the bullish claim of better profitability than in the past few years, it also reminds you that small changes in refining spreads can have an outsized impact on the bottom line.

Putting those trailing numbers next to the bullish narrative helps you see why some investors think HF Sinclair is on a better footing, but also why even small margin shifts can matter a lot here.

🐂 HF Sinclair Bull Case

Valuation signals pull in different directions

  • HF Sinclair trades at US$51.57 per share with a trailing P/E of 24.3x, compared with 14.5x for the wider US Oil & Gas industry and 19x for peers, while a DCF fair value of US$204.63 points to a very large gap between that model and the current price.
  • What stands out is the tension between a premium P/E multiple and that high DCF fair value, which both bulls and bears use differently, and the numbers give you a way to test each side rather than taking either at face value.
    • Supportive of the bullish angle, the combination of 18.7% trailing earnings growth and a current price far below the US$204.63 DCF fair value is exactly the sort of setup bulls point to when they say the market may not be paying fully for HF Sinclair’s cash flow potential.
    • In line with the cautious view, the 24.3x trailing P/E versus 14.5x for the industry and 19x for peers suggests the market is already assigning HF Sinclair a richer multiple than many competitors, which bears see as leaving less room for error, especially with a dividend yield of 3.88% that is not strongly covered by earnings.

data.title: Quad Graphics (QUAD) Return To Profitability Tests Bullish Margin Expansion Narrative
data.description:
Quad/Graphics (QUAD) closed out FY 2025 with fourth quarter revenue of $630.6 million and basic EPS of $0.23, alongside net income of $11.1 million. Over the past few quarters, the company has seen revenue move from $708.4 million and EPS of $0.10 in Q4 2024 through $629.4 million and $0.12 in Q1 2025, $571.9 million and roughly breakeven EPS in Q2 2025, then $588 million and $0.21 in Q3 2025. This frames a year where profitability settled in at a trailing twelve month EPS of $0.57 on $2.4 billion of revenue. For investors, the latest print points to margins that are back in positive territory, with the key question now around how durable that profitability proves to be.

See our full analysis for Quad/Graphics.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up with the major narratives around Quad/Graphics, and where the story the numbers tell might challenge those views.

See what the community is saying about Quad/Graphics

Advertisement

TTM profit of $27 million after prior losses

  • On a trailing twelve month basis, Quad/Graphics moved from a loss of $50.9 million at the end of Q4 2024 to net income of $27 million by Q4 2025. Over the same period, TTM EPS shifted from a loss of $1.07 per share to earnings of $0.57 per share.
  • What stands out against the bullish view that earnings quality is improving is the mix of factors behind this swing. The data cites both multi year earnings growth of 13.4% a year and a $21.8 million one off loss in the last twelve months, so investors need to separate:
    • The core pattern of quarterly net income turning positive in 2025, from $5.8 million in Q1 to $11.1 million in Q4, which supports the idea of a more profitable business model.
    • The impact of that one off $21.8 million charge in the TTM figures, which affects how clean the earnings trend really is when you apply the bullish margin expansion story.

Bulls argue that this move into positive TTM earnings could be the foundation for a stronger long term profit story, and you can see how that thinking lines up with the detailed narrative here: 🐂 Quad/Graphics Bull Case

P/E of 12.5x versus higher industry multiples

  • The company is trading on a trailing P/E of 12.5x, compared with 25.1x for the US Commercial Services industry and 31.6x for the peer average. A DCF fair value of US$16.33 sits well above the current share price of US$6.63.
  • Analysts' consensus narrative sees Quad/Graphics using AI driven automation and integrated marketing to grow higher margin services, and the current numbers partly support that value angle yet still leave questions, because:
    • The apparent gap between the share price of US$6.63, the DCF fair value of US$16.33 and the analyst target of US$9.10 points to the market pricing the stock below both modelled cash flow value and the consensus price target.
    • At the same time, TTM revenue of about US$2.4b at Q4 2025 compares with US$2.7b at Q3 2024, so the business is working off a lower sales base while trying to shift mix toward data driven marketing solutions.

Interest coverage and debt temper the bull case

  • One of the clearest risks in the data is that interest payments are not well covered by earnings, alongside a reported US$193 million of debt maturing in 2029 at a blended interest rate of 7.2% and an unstable dividend track record.
  • Bears focus on that financing pressure plus ongoing print headwinds, and the reported figures give some grounding to that cautious stance because:
    • Even with TTM net income of US$27 million by Q4 2025, the combination of interest costs and a past one off loss of US$21.8 million means cash flow has less room to absorb any setback in revenue or margins.
    • Recent revenue prints, from US$708.4 million in Q4 2024 to US$630.6 million in Q4 2025, show the business working through a smaller top line while still carrying that interest burden and credit investors' attention on leverage.

data.title: EVA Live launches client-facing NeuroServer web app to support enterprise growth
data.description:
Ad-tech company EVA Live (Nasdaq:GOAI) has launched its first client-facing web application for NeuroServer, its proprietary AI-powered ad server, marking what management describes as the transition into full commercial execution.

While framed as a product launch, the development carries broader implications for investors. The new web interface turns NeuroServer from a backend AI engine into a full enterprise platform, giving advertisers a centralized environment to launch, manage, and optimize campaigns in real time.

The company says early adoption has already begun following the beta release of NeuroServer two months ago, and as stated in yesterday’s press release, management expects the web application to become a “key growth driver and primary revenue engine throughout 2026 and beyond.”

Advertisement

Why the web application matters more than it sounds

NeuroServer itself has been in development for several years. The introduction of a structured, client-facing web application changes the commercial dynamic.

Instead of relying solely on direct integrations or managed-service workflows, EVA Live now offers advertisers a scalable interface that centralizes onboarding, campaign management, performance monitoring, and optimization.

From an investor’s perspective, this matters for three reasons:

  1. Scalability : A standardized web interface reduces friction in onboarding new clients and makes it easier to scale account volume without expanding headcount proportionally.
  2. Operating leverage : Automation reduces manual optimization work, potentially supporting margin expansion as revenue grows.
  3. Recurring revenue potential : A client-facing enterprise platform increases switching costs and creates the foundation for recurring revenue relationships.

The company said its real-time AI engine continuously analyzes performance data to optimize campaigns, mitigate fraud, and adjust targeting dynamically. Early client results indicate the system can increase ROI by up to 40%, according to management, though outcomes are likely to vary across customers.

The exact percentage is less important than the direction of travel. If clients see measurable performance improvements, retention and budget expansion typically follow.

Built for enterprise scale

Robert Vacaro, lead engineer on the NeuroServer web application, said the infrastructure was designed to support large advertisers managing significant campaign volumes.

“Our infrastructure is designed specifically for large-scale advertisers, enabling us to grow rapidly while delivering consistent performance and measurable results,” Vacaro said.

For investors, this signals an ambition beyond niche optimization tools. EVA Live is positioning NeuroServer as enterprise-grade infrastructure capable of handling significant advertising volumes.

The company said it is currently onboarding new online advertisers daily, suggesting early traction following the beta release.

Connecting product maturity to financial discipline

The launch also helps contextualize EVA Live’s recent capital allocation decisions, including the withdrawal of a planned equity raise.

A company still in heavy development mode often requires external funding to support product buildout. A company entering full commercial execution can begin focusing on scaling revenue from existing infrastructure.

“With the NeuroServer now live, we are shifting from development into full commercialization,” management said in a recent update.

If the platform scales as intended, revenue growth may increasingly depend on client acquisition and retention rather than product R&D cycles.

What investors should watch next

The launch itself is a milestone, but execution will matter more than messaging.

Investors will likely look for:

  1. Evidence of new client growth
  2. Data around recurring revenue contribution
  3. Indications for margins as campaign volume scales
  4. Customer retention and budget expansion metrics

If NeuroServer’s web application succeeds in turning performance gains into long-term enterprise relationships, EVA Live’s growth profile could shift toward a more predictable, software-like model.

About EVA Live

EVA Live is an AI-driven digital advertising company focused on delivering scalable performance marketing infrastructure. Following its Nasdaq uplisting in January 2026, the company has positioned NeuroServer as its core revenue platform for enterprise advertisers.

data.title: ALEC Holdings PJSC's (DFM:ALEC) Earnings May Just Be The Starting Point
data.description:
ALEC Holdings PJSC's (DFM:ALEC) strong earnings report was rewarded with a positive stock price move. We have done some analysis, and we found several positive factors beyond the profit numbers.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

Zooming In On ALEC Holdings PJSC's Earnings

As finance nerds would already know, the accrual ratio from cashflow is a key measure for assessing how well a company's free cash flow (FCF) matches its profit. In plain english, this ratio subtracts FCF from net profit, and divides that number by the company's average operating assets over that period. The ratio shows us how much a company's profit exceeds its FCF.

Therefore, it's actually considered a good thing when a company has a negative accrual ratio, but a bad thing if its accrual ratio is positive. While it's not a problem to have a positive accrual ratio, indicating a certain level of non-cash profits, a high accrual ratio is arguably a bad thing, because it indicates paper profits are not matched by cash flow. To quote a 2014 paper by Lewellen and Resutek, "firms with higher accruals tend to be less profitable in the future".

Over the twelve months to December 2025, ALEC Holdings PJSC recorded an accrual ratio of -0.53. That implies it has very good cash conversion, and that its earnings in the last year actually significantly understate its free cash flow. Indeed, in the last twelve months it reported free cash flow of د.إ966m, well over the د.إ686.2m it reported in profit. ALEC Holdings PJSC shareholders are no doubt pleased that free cash flow improved over the last twelve months.

That might leave you wondering what analysts are forecasting in terms of future profitability. Luckily, you can click here to see an interactive graph depicting future profitability, based on their estimates.

Our Take On ALEC Holdings PJSC's Profit Performance

As we discussed above, ALEC Holdings PJSC's accrual ratio indicates strong conversion of profit to free cash flow, which is a positive for the company. Based on this observation, we consider it possible that ALEC Holdings PJSC's statutory profit actually understates its earnings potential! Better yet, its EPS are growing strongly, which is nice to see. Of course, we've only just scratched the surface when it comes to analysing its earnings; one could also consider margins, forecast growth, and return on investment, among other factors. While it's really important to consider how well a company's statutory earnings represent its true earnings power, it's also worth taking a look at what analysts are forecasting for the future. Luckily, you can check out what analysts are forecasting by clicking here.

Approach 1: LVMH Moët Hennessy - Louis Vuitton Société Européenne Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company might be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to a present value.

For LVMH Moët Hennessy - Louis Vuitton Société Européenne, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections out to 2035. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about €13.1b. Analyst inputs cover the nearer years, and Simply Wall St extrapolates further out, with projected free cash flow of €12.8b in 2029. Approach 2: LVMH Moët Hennessy - Louis Vuitton Société Européenne Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful shorthand because it links what you pay per share directly to the earnings the business is already generating. It lets you quickly see how many euros of price you are paying for each euro of current earnings.

What counts as a "normal" P/E depends on how the market views the company’s growth prospects and risk. In general, higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher multiple, while lower growth or higher risk usually line up with a lower P/E.

LVMH currently trades on a P/E of 24.36x. That is above the Luxury industry average of about 17.14x, but below the peer group average of 52.18x. As path to profitability is the topic on Fluence's investors mind, we've decided to gauge market sentiment. Expectations from some of the Australian Water Utilities analysts is that Fluence is on the verge of breakeven. At what rate will the company have to grow in order to realise the consensus estimates forecasting breakeven in under 12 months? Using a line of best fit, we calculated an average annual growth rate of 113%, which signals high confidence from analysts. If this rate turns out to be too aggressive, the company may become profitable much later than analysts predict.

data.title: Analysts Have Lowered Expectations For PeptiDream Inc. (TSE:4587) After Its Latest Results
data.description:
There's been a notable change in appetite for PeptiDream Inc. (TSE:4587) shares in the week since its yearly report, with the stock down 11% to JP¥1,388. The results look positive overall; while revenues of JP¥19b were in line with analyst predictions, statutory losses were 6.7% smaller than expected, with PeptiDream losing JP¥28.99 per share. The analysts typically update their forecasts at each earnings report, and we can judge from their estimates whether their view of the company has changed or if there are any new concerns to be aware of. So we gathered the latest post-earnings forecasts to see what estimates suggest is in store for next year.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

After the latest results, the seven analysts covering PeptiDream are now predicting revenues of JP¥47.9b in 2026. If met, this would reflect a huge 159% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. PeptiDream is also expected to turn profitable, with statutory earnings of JP¥127 per share. Yet prior to the latest earnings, the analysts had been anticipated revenues of JP¥56.1b and earnings per share (EPS) of JP¥156 in 2026. Indeed, we can see that the analysts are a lot more bearish about PeptiDream's prospects following the latest results, administering a real cut to revenue estimates and slashing their EPS estimates to boot.

Check out our latest analysis for PeptiDream

The analysts made no major changes to their price target of JP¥3,008, suggesting the downgrades are not expected to have a long-term impact on PeptiDream's valuation. Fixating on a single price target can be unwise though, since the consensus target is effectively the average of analyst price targets. As a result, some investors like to look at the range of estimates to see if there are any diverging opinions on the company's valuation. There are some variant perceptions on PeptiDream, with the most bullish analyst valuing it at JP¥4,400 and the most bearish at JP¥2,700 per share. This shows there is still a bit of diversity in estimates, but analysts don't appear to be totally split on the stock as though it might be a success or failure situation.

Another way we can view these estimates is in the context of the bigger picture, such as how the forecasts stack up against past performance, and whether forecasts are more or less bullish relative to other companies in the industry. It's clear from the latest estimates that PeptiDream's rate of growth is expected to accelerate meaningfully, with the forecast 159% annualised revenue growth to the end of 2026 noticeably faster than its historical growth of 22% p.a. over the past five years. By contrast, our data suggests that other companies (with analyst coverage) in a similar industry are forecast to grow their revenue at 27% per year. It seems obvious that, while the growth outlook is brighter than the recent past, the analysts also expect PeptiDream to grow faster than the wider industry.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The most important thing to take away is that the analysts downgraded their earnings per share estimates, showing that there has been a clear decline in sentiment following these results. Regrettably, they also downgraded their revenue estimates, but the latest forecasts still imply the business will grow faster than the wider industry. The consensus price target held steady at JP¥3,008, with the latest estimates not enough to have an impact on their price targets.

Following on from that line of thought, we think that the long-term prospects of the business are much more relevant than next year's earnings. We have estimates - from multiple PeptiDream analysts - going out to 2028, and you can see them free on our platform here.

Don't forget that there may still be risks. For instance, we've identified 1 warning sign for PeptiDream that you should be aware of.
